problem_statement: `collectBoundChildren`, `tyVarChildFilter`, and `boundFlexChildren` currently exist in both `src/MLF/Binding/Tree.hs` and `src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs`, creating split ownership for the same bound-child query logic and increasing the risk of thesis-faithfulness drift when one copy changes without the other.
thesis_alignment: This is a helper-ownership consolidation only: keep the paper-defined Q(n) behavior unchanged, with `MLF.Binding.Tree` remaining the single owner of direct flexibly-bound TyVar child queries used by binding-tree operations. No semantic deviation from `papers/these-finale-english.txt` is intended; `papers/xmlf.txt` is only a fallback reference if the thesis is silent, which does not appear necessary for this helper move.
root_cause: The validation module retained local copies of bound-child query helpers after validation logic was split out of `MLF.Binding.Tree`, instead of importing the canonical helpers from the tree module or a single shared helper location. That left two live implementations of the same thesis-facing query path.
step_by_step_tasks: Add a narrow regression/guard in `test/BindingSpec.hs` that exercises the bound-child query path through validation-adjacent behavior, so the refactor preserves the existing binder-selection invariant before moving code.
Promote `MLF.Binding.Tree` as the sole owner of `collectBoundChildren`, `tyVarChildFilter`, and `boundFlexChildren`, keeping exports and local helper structure thesis-exact and minimal.
Delete the duplicated helper definitions from `src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs` and switch validation code to import/use the `MLF.Binding.Tree` ownership path without introducing new fallback logic.
Update `CHANGELOG.md` with a concise maintenance entry noting the binding-helper consolidation, since this is meaningful internal simplification work.
Run targeted validation first on the binding spec that covers `boundFlexChildren`/binding invariants, then run the full verification gate `cabal build all && cabal test`.
tests_and_commands: Inspect current duplication and ownership boundaries with `rg -n "collectBoundChildren|tyVarChildFilter|boundFlexChildren" src/MLF/Binding/Tree.hs src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs`.
Run focused regression coverage first with `cabal test mlf2-test --test-options='--match "boundFlexChildren"'`.
If the new guard is phrased under binding-tree validation instead, use the narrowest matching selector in `test/BindingSpec.hs` for that example.
Run the full gate with `cabal build all && cabal test`.
acceptance_criteria: There is exactly one implementation of `collectBoundChildren`, `tyVarChildFilter`, and `boundFlexChildren` in the codebase, owned by `src/MLF/Binding/Tree.hs`.
`src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs` no longer contains duplicate helper bodies for those queries.
Existing and/or newly added focused binding tests pass without changing observable thesis-defined behavior.
The full Cabal build and test gate passes.
Documentation/changelog reflects the simplification if a code change lands.
abort_criteria: The refactor introduces an import cycle between `MLF.Binding.Tree` and `MLF.Binding.Validation` that cannot be resolved with a minimal ownership-preserving change.
A focused regression shows that validation relied on subtly different helper semantics than tree operations, implying the duplication masked a real behavior split rather than redundant code.
Review of the thesis or existing tests indicates the helper move would alter Q(n) semantics or binder ordering rather than just consolidate ownership.
The required fix expands beyond bounded helper consolidation into broader module reorganization.
good_enough_definition: Duplication is removed with the smallest possible edit surface.
Ownership is explicit: bound-child query helpers live in `MLF.Binding.Tree`, and validation only consumes them.
A focused regression check exists or is adjusted to guard the moved ownership invariant.
Targeted validation passes before the full Cabal gate.
No paper-visible behavior changes are introduced.
